rulings.Case Title: JACK OLSON VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Sever And Stay The Walsh, Olson, And Similarly-Situated Cases Pending Resolution Of Crull Appeal
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant 3M Company¿s motion to sever and stay the Walsh, Olson, and similarly-situated cases pending resolution of Crull appeal: hearing required. At the hearing the parties will be asked their views whether it would be efficacious to sever 3M from all cases currently pending in this court where liability is based solely on use of 3M masks and then to consolidate all of those cases for all pretrial and trial purposes with a trial date to be set early in 2012. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOSEPH DAZZO VS. ALLIED PACKING & SUPPLY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Ford Motor Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1 (negligence), 2 (strict liability), 3 (failure to warn), 4 (breach of warranty), and 7 (survival) and granted as to issues 5 (fraud and conspiracy), 6 (false representation) and 8 (punitive damages). Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Dazzo was exposed to asbestos-containing brakes attributable to defendant. Diana Dazzo¿s deposition testimony and defendant¿s responses to plaintiffs¿ special interrogatories and requests for admission preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that any exposure to an asbestos-containing product for which defendant is liable was not a substantial factor in causing or contributing to Mr. Dazzo¿s mesothelioma. As to issue 4, defendant did not satisfy its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Dazzo was in privity with defendant As to issues 5 and 6, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant made any misrepresentation to Mr. Dazzo or committed any fraudulent concealment. As to issue 8, defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs did not present evidence creating a triable issue whether defendant acted with malice, fraud or oppression by clear and convincing evidence. Plaintiffs¿ objections to the deposition testimony of Dr. Hammar are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SHERMAN FERGUSON et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To File 2nd Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 2. Plaintiffs¿ motion for leave to file second amended complaint is granted. No opposition filed. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD HARRAH VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B P) AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Groeniger & Company¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its motion for summary adjudication of false representation and punitive damages is off calendar. Defendant did not sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing pipe supplied by defendant. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony is not contradictory to his testimony in his Workers¿ Compensation case and, even if it was, there is a serious question whether the D¿Amico rule would apply in this situation. Plaintiff¿s objections to Mr. Groeniger¿s declaration are sustained. Even if Mr. Groeniger¿s declaration was admissible, it merely creates a triable issue whether defendant supplied asbestos-containing pipe to plaintiff¿s jobsites. Summary adjudication is taken off calendar because plaintiff represents that he is dismissing his claims for false representation and punitive damages, the only two issues defendant sought to be summarily adjudicated. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LESTER ELIS et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 5. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production that plaintiffs do not have and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Ellis was exposed to Unibestos attributable to defendant and plaintiffs have not presented admissible evidence creating a triable issue. Defendant¿s objections to the declarations of Mr. Ellis and Mr. Kenneth Cohen are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: RAYMOND BARRAGAN VS. THOMAS DEE ENGINEERING COMPANY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Rockwell Animation'S Mo To Dismiss Allegations Related To Asbestosis And Asbestos-Related Pleural Disease
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Thursday, September 1, 2011 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Scott Co. of California¿s joinder to defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to dismiss allegations related to asbestosis is off calendar as moot as plaintiff filed a dismissal of his claims based on asbestosis and plaintiff represents that he is not pursuing, and has not ever pursued, a claim based on asbestos-related pleural disease. = (220/HEK)
